title: POV: You're the World's First Trillionaire (It Owns You)
hook: You wake up and the number in your account has thirteen digits — more money than any human has ever held. You're the world's first trillionaire. And you're about to learn it cost you everything that made you a person.
body: POV: you're the world's first trillionaire. You live it in second person, present tense, one number at a time, as the fortune climbs and the person underneath it hollows out. $0: your card declines for four dollars of coffee, your sister Dani is sick in the way that costs money you don't have, and the only want you have left is to get enough that the word 'no' stops meaning her. $1 MILLION: the first wire clears at 2 a.m. and you pay her whole hospital bill in one click. $100 MILLION: you stop being a person with a company and become an institution with a person attached. $1 BILLION: to cross it, you gut what your best friend and co-founder Sam built and end the town that raised you — and she walks out the same garage door you started in. $100 BILLION: you say one word and three markets flinch, and you can no longer tell a friend from a position. $1 TRILLION: the world's first, worth more than most nations — and your sister is calling, live, and your hands are full of champagne. Then the reckoning: you learn a trillion dollars is not money you can spend. It's a stock position that only stays real if you never touch it. Real, verified near-future wealth mechanics woven into the story: a trillion seconds is about 31,700 years while a billion is only 32; spending a million dollars a day since the birth of Christ still wouldn't reach a trillion; a mega-fortune is unrealized equity that craters if you try to sell it; 'buy, borrow, die' — the ultra-rich borrow against their shares and pay no income tax; a paper fortune can swell by more than a million dollars a minute; and a net worth set entirely by other people, second by second. No trillionaire exists yet — this is an explicitly near-future, dramatized cautionary POV about wealth, power, and isolation. Watch the whole series — every life, every level, every way the number wins. For educational and entertainment purposes; characters are fictional, the mechanics are real.
tags: what if you were a trillionaire
how much is a trillion dollars
pinned: A trillion seconds is about 31,700 years. A billion is only 32. That's the whole trap — they sound alike and they are nothing alike. Which level do YOU think the money would have taken you: $1 MILLION, $1 BILLION, or all the way to $1 TRILLION?
poll: Where does the number get you? $1M (you stop) / $1B (you cut the town) / $100B (you can't tell who's real) / $1T (it owns you)
